prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design a panel of IL-2 sequences from Homo sapiens (PDB 2B5I) with improved IL-2Rβγ receptor selectivity over IL-2Rα to reduce regulatory T-cell expansion in cancer immunotherapy applications. Apply partial sequence design to the IL-2Rα-binding face while preserving IL-2Rβγ contact geometry. outline a computational approach covering: (1) chain-level mask: design IL-2Rα interface residues only (positions 35–43, 61–65), (2) ProteinMPNN sampling (temperature 0.2) with 500 sequences, (3) interface energy scoring with Rosetta InterfaceAnalyzer for IL-2Rα vs IL-2Rβγ, (4) top-20 candidates for bacterial expression and SPR binding kinetics. Discuss implications for CD25-low versus CD25-high T-cell activation ratios.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (2B5I); target: IL-2; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0
